items.q: What is the structural framework of The Canterbury Tales?
items.correct: A frame narrative — pilgrims travelling to Canterbury tell stories along the way, allowing Chaucer to juxtapose different voices, genres and social classes.
items.misconception: It is one continuous story told by Chaucer from beginning to end.
items.random_wrong: It is a religious text used in Catholic services.
items.why: Students miss the frame structure. Chaucer's strategy is polyphony — voicing many speakers to compare and contrast — not a single narrative voice.

items.q: What defines epic poetry as a genre?
items.correct: Long narrative poems on heroic subjects, in elevated style, often invoking a muse, in medias res openings, and treating events of national or cosmic significance.
items.misconception: Epic means any poem that is very long.
items.random_wrong: Epic means any story with a battle in it.
items.why: Length alone isn't enough. Epic has specific conventions (invocation, elevated diction, heroic subject) inherited from Homer through Virgil to Milton — students reduce it to scale.

items.correct: When the audience knows something a character does not, creating tension between the character's words/actions and the audience's understanding.
items.misconception: Dramatic irony is when something ironic happens in a play.
items.random_wrong: Dramatic irony is when characters speak in dramatic voices.
items.why: Students treat 'dramatic irony' as just irony in a drama. The defining feature is the gap between audience and character knowledge — without that gap, it isn't dramatic irony.

items.q: Propp identified how many character spheres?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 7. “12” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Propp's 'Morphology of the Folktale' was published in:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1928. “1949” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Propp identified how many narrative functions?
items.why: The real figure is 31. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
